titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the International Imperial Court System Collection
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday September 24, 2022 at 03:20

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of programs, fliers and handbills, tickets, leaflets, certificates, medals, pins, and other ephemera and memorabilia related to the LGBTQ group the International Court System (ICS), also known as the International Imperial Court System. Materials primarily document activities of individual courts in California and the Pacific Northwest in the 1970s and 1980s, including annual Coronation Balls, drag shows, and other charitable or social events.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Ephemera and Papers, 1971-2008. II. Pins and Medals, 1973-2003.

bioghist.head: International Imperial Court System
bioghist.p: The International Court System (ICS), also known as the International Imperial Court System, is one of the oldest and largest LGBTQ organizations in the world. The court system was founded in San Francisco in 1965 by José Sarria, the first openly gay candidate for public office in the United States. In 1962, Sarria and a group of gay bar owners formed the Tavern Guild in response to police harassment of their businesses. In 1963, the Guild held the Beaux Arts Costume Ball, the first large public drag ball in San Francisco; several years later Sarria was crowned Queen of the Ball, and after he declared himself Empress, the coronation became tradition and the court system developed. As of 2022, the ICS consists of a network of more than 65 individual charitable nonprofit organizations in North America, with the Imperial Council of San Francisco considered the "Mother Court." Each court, or chapter, conducts fundraising events throughout the year to support LGBTQ and other causes, the most notable being their annual Coronation Balls. Courts generally crown two monarchs during Coronation Balls who serve for a year and help stage charity events that often feature drag performers. Most courts are known as empires, although some are designated baronies or ducal courts; the latter is used when a chapter's geographical territory overlaps with an established court, such as the Ducal Court of San Francisco.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: International Imperial Court System Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of programs, fliers and handbills, tickets, leaflets, certificates, medals, pins, and other ephemera and memorabilia related to the LGBTQ group the International Court System (ICS), also known as the International Imperial Court System. Materials primarily document activities of individual courts in California and the Pacific Northwest in the 1970s and 1980s, including annual Coronation Balls, drag shows, and other charitable or social events. Among the courts represented are the Imperial Council of San Francisco, Grand Ducal Council of San Francisco, Imperial Sovereign Rose Court of Oregon, and the Imperial Sovereign Court of the Raintree Empire (Washington). Files for the Rose Court include a guide to Portland's court system by Rose Emperor VI Tom Terrific. Raintree Empire records contain handwritten speeches by Empress III Ronni Bee and Emperor III Jim, as well as copies of the group's articles of incorporation and bylaws, bank records, circular letters, mailing lists, and a miniature leather vest. The collection further contains programs for the Tavern Guild of San Francisco's Beaux Arts Ball and events of other organizations affiliated with the ICS, including the gay motorcycle clubs Barbary Coasters and Inter-Club Fund of San Francisco, and the Black LGBTQ groups United Ebony Council of Seattle and United Ebony Kingdom (Portland, Oregon). Also present are certificates and medals given to winners of the Billy DeFrank Camp Awards; general ephemera such as a pamphlet on venereal disease issued by the Gay Community Center of Seattle and a National Gay Task Force Women's Caucus brochure; and newspapers serving LGBTQ communities, including the NW Fountain and Gay Times .

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The International Imperial Court System Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Grand Ducal Council of San Francisco
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Raintree Empire
Imperial Sovereign Rose Court of Oregon
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force (U.S.)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by state or country and court, with materials not identified with a particular ICS chapter filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily contains programs for annual Coronation Balls held by chapters of the International Court System, in addition to related fliers, handbills, leaflets, tickets, and other ephemera. Programs typically feature messages from current monarchs, campaign ads by candidates running for monarch, photographs of drag performers and other balls or parties, and gay and gay-friendly business advertising. Included are a small amount of administrative and financial papers of the Imperial Sovereign Court of the Raintree Empire and handwritten speeches by Empress Ronni Bee and Emperor Jim. The series also contains materials related to events and organizations such as the San Francisco Beaux Arts Ball, Closet Ball, Billy DeFrank Camp Awards, Barbary Coasters, Inter-Club Fund of San Francisco, United Ebony Council of Seattle, and United Ebony Kingdom. Also present are general ephemera concerning the LGBTQ community.

did.unittitle: Court of Wine and Roses (Sonoma County)
Grand Ducal Council of San Francisco
"The history of the Grand Ducal Council de San Francisco," typed supplement to Grand Ducal Ball program
Imperial Casa de San Jose Empire
Souvenirs of Emperor Hector's Tribute to Emperor Norton
Imperial Court of Los Angeles and Hollywood
Imperial Court of Sacramento (Court of the Great Northwest Imperial Empire)
Imperial High Court of the Peninsula
International Imperial Court of Long Beach
"A Fellini Fantasy" - Coronation Ball program, schedule, and tickets
Imperial Barony of Winnipeg and All of Manitoba
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Chinook Arch
Imperial Court of the Rocky Mountain Empire
Imperial Royal Sovereign Court of the Desert Empire
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Emerald Empire
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Willamette Empire
Imperial Sovereign Rose Court of Oregon
"I Love Portland: A Guide to the Portland Court System and its Organization" by Tom Terrific
"I Am…I Said," campaign brochure by Tom Terrific (Rose Emperor VI)
Royal Court of the Golden Spike Empire
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Evergreen Empire
Imperial Sovereign Court of the Raintree Empire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Awards and recognition granted to and by Raintree monarchs including Ronni Bee, Gart, and Summer Breeze.

did.unittitle: Fliers, tickets, program supplements, and circular letters
Invitations and circulars from other courts
H.I.M. Tree Empress III Ronni Bee and H.I.M. Rain Emperor III Jim: autograph manuscript speeches, business card, typed letter by Ronni Bee, order of events for Coronation Ball

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Raintree Empire" is handwritten in ink on front lapels. The back of the vest features a drawing of half an evergreen or spruce tree, with 1978/79 written vertically down one side, and "98660" on the other side. "Van, Wash" is written below.

did.unittitle: Imperial Sovereign Court of Seattle & the Olympic and Rainier Empire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes references to multiple California courts, coronations, and LGBTQ charitable events. AIC&O may have been an umbrella organization affiliated with the ICS; Billy DeFrank was president.

did.unittitle: The Crown Princess Presentation Ball program
"Don't Give Him Anything But Love: What You Should Know About VD," pamphlet created by the Gay Community Center, Seattle
Inter-Club Fund of San Francisco "Casualty Capers" and "On the Town" programs
"The Maltese Cross," monthly publication of the Knights of Malta - Jet Chapter, Seattle
United Ebony Council of Seattle Celebration '79 program
Various fliers and leaflets, some unidentified

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of the Metropolis Gayzette and flier for a Nina Hagen concert.

did.unittitle: Various tickets and cards, some unidentified
Women's Caucus of the National Gay Task Force brochure

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of pinback buttons and medals commemorating Coronation Balls and campaigns for monarchs of the International Court System and the United Ebony Kingdom, as well as the Billy DeFrank Camp Awards Ball. Among the courts represented are the Imperial Council of San Francisco, Imperial Court de San Diego, Owl Empire of Stanislaus County, Imperial Dove Court de Fresno, Imperial Sovereign Court of the Raintree Empire, Imperial Sovereign Rose Court of Oregon, Silver Dollar Court, Royal Court of the Golden Spike Empire, and Imperial Sovereign Court of the Chinook Arch. A few pieces are made of enamel or glass and designed in shapes such as a heart or pair of red lips.

did.unittitle: Inkjet printout of photographs showing selected pins and medals
